title: Coleataenia abscissa
intro: Coleataenia abscissa is a species of flowering plant in the grass family, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses in various cultures. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. Bioactive constituents of Coleataenia abscissa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Coleataenia abscissa is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Coleataenia, which is a group of grasses native to the Americas. The genus Coleataenia is part of the subfamily Maydeae, which includes various species of grasses. Within the subfamily Maydeae, Coleataenia abscissa is further classified as a member of the tribe Maydeae. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
